name: CAC Score
description: Coronary artery calcification (CAC) score using Agatston scoring of CT scan(s) of coronary arteries
example_variables: MD1CAC_S: CAC SCORE IN WHOLE HEART

name: CAC Volume
description: Coronary artery calcium volume using CT scan(s) of coronary arteries
example_variables: volpm1c: MEAN: TOTAL CALCIUM VOLUME, PHANTOM-ADJUSTED
volpmfc: MEAN: TOTAL CALCIUM VOLUME, PHANTOM-ADJUSTED

name: Carotid Plaque
description: Presence or absence of carotid plaque.
example_variables: imt_anyplaque_baseline: Any presence of carotid plaque as measured by IMT at baseline visit
PLAQUE01: Plaque (with or without shadowing) in any carotid site
LMRPH141: YEAR 5 LESION MORPHOLOGY, RT SIDE

name: Carotid Stenosis
description: Extent of narrowing of the carotid artery.
example_variables: PSTEN141: YEAR 5 PERCENT STENOSIS, RT SIDE
PSTEN241: YEAR 5 PERCENT STENOSIS, LFT SIDE
PSTEN155: BL REREAD PERCENT STENOSIS, RT SIDE

name: CIMT
description: Common carotid intima-media thickness, calculated as the mean of two values: mean of multiple thickness estimates from the left far wall and from the right far wall.
example_variables: imt_mean_farwall_cca_baseline: Carotid intima-media far wall thickness at baseline visit
LOPAAV45: Average far wall width, left common carotid: optimal angle
LANAAV45: Average far wall width, left common carotid: anterior angle

name: CIMT
description: Common carotid intima-media thickness, calculated as the mean of four values: maximum of multiple thickness estimates from the left far wall, left near wall, right far wall, and right near wall.
example_variables: LOPAMX23: Maximum near wall width, left common carotid: optimal angle
LANAMX23: Maximum near wall width, left common carotid: anterior angle
LPOAMX23: Maximum near wall width, left common carotid: posterior angle

name: Angina Incident
description: An indicator of whether a subject had an angina event (that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals) during the follow-up period.

name: CABG Incident
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) procedure (that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals) during the follow-up period.
example_variables: cvddate: Date of Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) Status

name: Cad Followup Start Age
description: Age of subject at the start of the cardiovascular disease (CAD/CHD) follow-up period during which atherosclerosis events were reviewed and adjudicated. Only match when context explicitly mentions cardiovascular follow-up or event surveillance — do NOT match generic enrollment or baseline age.
example_variables: AGEBL: Calculated age at baseline, start of cardiovascular follow-up
cad_followup_start_age: Age at start of CAD event surveillance

name: Chd Death Definite
description: An indicator of whether the cause of death was determined by medical professionals or technicians to be "definite" coronary heart disease for subjects who died during the follow-up period.

name: Chd Death Probable
description: An indicator of whether the cause of death was determined by medical professionals or technicians to be "probable" or "definite" coronary heart disease for subjects who died during the follow-up period.

name: Coronary Angioplasty Incident
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a coronary angioplasty procedure (that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals) during the follow-up period.

name: Mi Incident
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a myocardial infarction (MI) event (that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals) during the follow-up period.

name: Pad Incident
description: An indicator of whether a subject had peripheral arterial disease (that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals) during the follow-up period.

name: Angina Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had an angina event prior to the baseline visit.

name: CABG Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) procedure prior to the start of the baseline visit.
example_variables: PHEA07A: [Invasive procedures]. Have you ever had surgery on your heart, or on the arteries of your neck or legs, excluding surgery for vericose veins? Coronary bypass. Q7a
BPSSURBL: CORONARY BYPASS SURGERY AT BL

name: Coronary Angioplasty Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a coronary angioplasty procedure prior to the start of the baseline visit.
example_variables: PHEA09A: [Invasive procedures]. [Probe for type of procedure]. Angioplasty of coronary artery(ies)? Q9a
MHXA54A: Q54a. F. Invasive procedures. Ever had angioplasty of coronary arteries? [Visit 1]

name: Coronary Revascularization Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a coronary revascularization procedure prior to the start of the baseline visit. This includes angioplasty, CABG, and other coronary revascularization procedures.
example_variables: ARTERIES: Ever had coronary bypass surgery/angioplasty/or balloon dilation because arteries of the heart were blocked?

name: Mi Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a myocardial infarction (MI) prior to the start of the baseline visit.
example_variables: PREVMI05: Prevalent MI from ECG or medical history

name: Pad Prior
description: An indicator of whether a subject had peripheral arterial disease prior to the baseline visit.
example_variables: PAD02: Peripheral artery disease visit 1, definition 2 - same for both genders

name: Bmi Baseline
description: Body mass index calculated at baseline.
example_variables: BMI: Body Mass Index calculated from Height and Weight

name: Current Smoker Baseline
description: Indicates whether subject currently smokes cigarettes.
example_variables: current_smoker_baseline: Current smoking status of participant at baseline visit

name: Ever Smoker Baseline
description: Indicates whether subject ever regularly smoked cigarettes.
example_variables: EVER_SMOKED_CIGARETTES: Answer to "Have you ever smoked cigarettes?"
EverSmokedCig: Have you ever smoked cigarettes?

name: Height Baseline
description: Body height at baseline.

name: Weight Baseline
description: Body weight at baseline.

name: Basophil (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of basophils in the blood (bld).
example_variables: basophils_baseline: Basophils value at baseline visit

name: Eosinophil (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of eosinophils in the blood (bld).
example_variables: eosinophils_baseline: Eosinophils value at baseline visit

name: Hematocrit (Volume Fraction in Blood)
description: Measurement of hematocrit, the fraction of volume (vfr) of blood (bld) that is composed of red blood cells.
example_variables: hematocrit_baseline: Hematocrit value at baseline visit

name: Hemoglobin (Mass Concentration in Blood)
description: Measurement of mass per volume, or mass concentration (mcnc), of hemoglobin in the blood (bld).
example_variables: hemoglobin_baseline: Hemoglobin value at baseline visit

name: Lymphocyte (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of lymphocytes in the blood (bld).
example_variables: lymphocytes_baseline: Lymphocytes value at baseline visit

name: MCH Entmass RBC
description: Measurement of the average mass (entmass) of hemoglobin per red blood cell(rbc), known as mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH).

name: MCHc Mcnc RBC
description: Measurement of the mass concentration (mcnc) of hemoglobin in a given volume of packed red blood cells (rbc), known as mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC).
example_variables: B767: MCHC (MEAN CORPUSCULAR HEMOGLOBIN CONCENTRATION) (METPATH lab profile)
MCHC: Mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (sample type: whole blood)
hemoglobin_baseline: Hemoglobin value at baseline visit

name: MCV Entvol RBC
description: Measurement of the average volume (entvol) of red blood cells (rbc), known as mean corpuscular volume (MCV).
example_variables: HMTB13: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) (to nearest whole unit). Q13

name: Monocyte (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of monocytes in the blood (bld).
example_variables: monocytes_baseline: Monocytes value at baseline visit

name: Neutrophil (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of neutrophils in the blood (bld).
example_variables: neutrophils_baseline: Neutrophils value at baseline visit

name: Platelet (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of platelets in the blood (bld).
example_variables: platelet_count_baseline: Platelet count at baseline visit

name: PMV (Volume per Entity in Blood)
description: Measurement of the mean volume (entvol) of platelets in the blood (bld), known as mean platelet volume (MPV or PMV).
example_variables: MPV: Mean platelet volume (sample type: whole blood)

name: RBC (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of red blood cells in the blood (bld).
example_variables: CBC4: Red blood cell count, Rbc

name: RDW Ratio RBC
description: Measurement of the ratio of variation in width to the mean width of the red blood cell (rbc) volume distribution curve taken at +/- 1 CV, known as red cell distribution width (RDW).
example_variables: CBC11: Red cell distribution width, Rdw
RDW: Red cell distribution width (sample type: whole blood)

name: WBC (Number Concentration in Blood)
description: Count by volume, or number concentration (ncnc), of white blood cells in the blood (bld).
example_variables: CBC3: White blood cell count, Wbc

name: Antihypertensive Meds
description: Indicator for use of antihypertensive medication at the time of blood pressure measurement.
example_variables: xbpmeds: Meds: On specified BP medication

name: Blood Pressure - Diastolic
description: Resting diastolic blood pressure from the upper arm in a clinical setting.
example_variables: SBPA14: Q14. D. First blood pressure measurement. Diastolic (first BP) [Visit 1]

name: Blood Pressure - Systolic
description: Resting systolic blood pressure from the upper arm in a clinical setting.
example_variables: SBPA13: Q13. D. First blood pressure measurement. Systolic (first BP) [Visit 1]

name: Annotated Sex
description: Subject sex, as recorded by the study.

name: Geographic Site
description: Recruitment/field center, baseline clinic, or geographic region.

name: Hispanic or Latino Ethnicity
description: Indicator of reported Hispanic or Latino ethnicity.
example_variables: H700: WHICH OF THE FOLLOWING BEST DESCRIBES YOU - ETHNICITY HISPANIC OR LATINO

name: Hispanic Subgroup
description: classification of Hispanic/Latino background for Hispanic/Latino subjects where country or region of origin information is available
example_variables: hctryo1c: COUNTRY OR REGION OF HISPANIC ORIGIN
hctryofc: COUNTRY OR REGION OF HISPANIC ORIGIN

name: Race Us
description: Reported race of participant according to the United States administrative definition of race.

name: Subcohort
description: A distinct subgroup within a study, generally indicating subjects who share similar characteristics due to study design. Subjects may belong to only one subcohort.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Cd40
description: Cluster of differentiation 40 ligand (CD40) concentration in blood.

name: CRP
description: C-reactive protein (CRP) concentration in blood.
example_variables: crp_baseline: CRP value at baseline visit

name: Eselectin
description: E-selectin concentration in blood.

name: ICAM-1
description: Intercellular adhesion molecule 1 (ICAM1) concentration in blood.

name: Il10
description: Interleukin 10 (IL10) concentration in blood.

name: Il18
description: Interleukin 18 (IL18) concentration in blood.

name: Il1 Beta
description: Interleukin 1 beta (IL1b) concentration in blood.

name: IL-6
description: Interleukin 6 (IL6) concentration in blood.

name: Isoprostane 8 Epi Pgf2A
description: Isoprostane 8-epi-prostaglandin F2 alpha (8-epi-PGF2a) concentration in urine.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Lppla2 Act
description: Activity of lipoprotein-associated phospholipase A2 (LP-PLA2), also known as platelet-activating factor acetylhydrolase, measured in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Lppla2 Mass
description: Mass of lipoprotein-associated phospholipase A2 (LP-PLA2), also known as platelet-activating factor acetylhydrolase, measured in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Mcp1
description: Monocyte chemoattractant protein-1 (MCP1), also known as C-C motif chemokine ligand 2, concentration in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Mmp9
description: Matrix metalloproteinase 9 (MMP9) concentration in blood.

name: Mpo
description: Myeloperoxidase (MPO) concentration in blood.

name: Opg
description: Osteoprotegerin (OPG) concentration in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Pselectin
description: P-selectin concentration in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Tnfa
description: Tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNFa) concentration in blood.

name: Tnfa R1
description: Tumor necrosis factor alpha receptor 1 (TNFa-R1) concentration in blood.
example_variables: tnfri1: TUMOR NECROSIS FACTOR-A SOLUBLE RECEPTORS (pg/ml)

name: Tnfr2
description: Tumor necrosis factor receptor 2 (TNFR2) concentration in blood.
example_variables: idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Fasting Lipids
description: Indicates whether participant fasted for at least eight hours prior to blood draw to measure lipids phenotypes.
example_variables: FAST0802: Fasting time of 8 hours or more

name: HDL
description: Blood mass concentration of high-density lipoprotein cholesterol
example_variables: hdl_baseline: HDL value at baseline visit

name: LDL
description: Blood mass concentration of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol

name: Lipid Lowering Medication
description: Indicates whether participant was taking any lipid-lowering medication at blood draw to measure lipids phenotypes
example_variables: meds_LIPIDS: Taking any Lipid Lowering Medication?

name: Total Cholesterol
description: Blood mass concentration of total cholesterol
example_variables: totchol_baseline: Total cholesterol value at baseline visit
TCHSIU01: Total cholesterol in SI units

name: Triglycerides
description: Blood mass concentration of triglycerides
example_variables: trigs_baseline: Triglycerides value at baseline visit
TRGSIU01: Total triglycerides In SI units

name: Sleep Duration
description: Usual amount of time slept per day.
example_variables: RSE21: F. Sleep. Q21. Hours of sleep in past month
F67SLPHR: PAST MONTH AVG SLEEPING HOURS. Q 7

name: VTE Case Status
description: An indicator of whether a subject experienced a venous thromboembolism event (VTE) that was verified by adjudication or by medical professionals.

name: VTE Followup Start Age
description: Age of subject at the start of the follow up period during which venous thromboembolism (VTE) events were reviewed and adjudicated.

name: VTE Prior History
description: An indicator of whether a subject had a venous thromboembolism (VTE) event prior to the start of the medical review process (including self-reported events).
example_variables: vtpehx01: LITE : Self-report of DVT or PE at baseline
idtype: Framingham Heart Study cohort identifier

name: Food Frequency Questionnaire
description: Food frequency questionnaire (FFQ) data: frequency and quantity of specific food items consumed over a reference period. Includes individual food items, food groups, and serving counts.
example_variables: APPLE: FFQ: FRESH APPLES OR PEARS (1)

name: Nutrient Intake
description: Computed nutrient intake values derived from dietary assessments: total calories, macronutrients (protein, fat, carbohydrates, fiber), micronutrients (vitamins, minerals), fatty acid profiles, and cholesterol intake.
example_variables: DT_KCAL: DAILY DIETARY ESTIMATES OF CALORIES

name: Dietary Supplement Use
description: Use of dietary supplements including multivitamins, individual vitamins (A, B, C, D, E), minerals (calcium, iron, zinc), fish oil, herbal supplements, and other over-the-counter nutritional supplements.
example_variables: VITCCURR: VITAMIN C MG: CURRENTLY TAKING

name: Diet Quality Score
description: Composite dietary quality indices and scores: Healthy Eating Index (HEI), DASH diet score, Mediterranean diet score, Dietary Inflammatory Index, and other summary measures of overall diet quality.
example_variables: HEI_TOTAL: Healthy Eating Index total score

name: Genetic Principal Components
description: Principal component analysis (PCA) eigenvectors computed from genome-wide genotype data to capture population structure and genetic ancestry. Typically PC1 through PC10 or PC20.
example_variables: pc5: Principal component 5: reveals a north-to-south cline within Europeans

name: Admixture Proportions
description: Ancestry proportions estimated from admixture models (e.g., ADMIXTURE, STRUCTURE) showing the fraction of an individual's genome derived from each ancestral population (African, European, East Asian, South Asian, Native American).
example_variables: AFR: Percent African calculated from ADMIXTURE algorithm
EUR: Percent European calculated from ADMIXTURE algorithm
ANCESTRY: Ancestry based on principal component analysis

name: Liver Function
description: Liver function tests and hepatic biomarkers: alanine aminotransferase (ALT/SGPT), aspartate aminotransferase (AST/SGOT), total and direct bilirubin, serum albumin, gamma-glutamyl transferase (GGT), alkaline phosphatase (ALP), and liver enzyme panels.

name: Electrocardiogram
description: Electrocardiogram (ECG/EKG) measurements and findings: QT interval, PR interval, QRS duration, heart rhythm, ST segment changes, P/Q/R/S/T wave morphology, axis deviation, atrial fibrillation, arrhythmia detection, and 12-lead ECG data.

name: Kidney Function
description: Kidney function tests and renal biomarkers: estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR), creatinine clearance, blood urea nitrogen (BUN), urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR), proteinuria, cystatin C, and dialysis status.
example_variables: UACR: Urine albumin to creatinine ratio

name: Heart Rate
description: Resting heart rate or pulse rate measured in a clinical setting, from ECG, or by pulse oximetry. Includes resting, exercise, and recovery heart rate.
example_variables: RHR: Resting heart rate from ECG

name: Cognition
description: Cognitive and neuropsychological test results: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE), Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), Digit Symbol Substitution Test, Trail Making Test, word recall and word list memory, clock drawing test, verbal fluency, and global cognitive scores.
example_variables: MMSE: Mini-Mental State Examination total score
DSST: Digit Symbol Substitution Test score

name: Hospitalization
description: Healthcare utilization events: hospital admissions, emergency room visits, ICU stays, length of stay, discharge disposition, readmission, inpatient days, and overnight hospitalizations.

name: Family Medical History
description: Family history of diseases and medical conditions: whether parents, siblings, or other relatives were diagnosed with specific conditions such as heart disease, cancer, diabetes, stroke, hypertension, or other heritable diseases.
example_variables: FH_MI: Family history of myocardial infarction

name: Surgical Procedure
description: Surgical procedures and operations: cataract surgery, joint replacement, appendectomy, tonsillectomy, organ transplant, biopsy, cardiac catheterization, and other invasive clinical procedures.

name: Diabetes Medication
description: Use of antidiabetic and glucose-lowering medications: metformin, sulfonylureas (glipizide, glyburide), insulin therapy, thiazolidinediones, DPP-4 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists, SGLT2 inhibitors, and oral hypoglycemic agents.

name: Anticoagulant Medication
description: Use of anticoagulant and blood-thinning medications: warfarin (Coumadin), heparin, enoxaparin (Lovenox), direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs: apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran), and antiplatelet therapy (clopidogrel).

name: Pain Medication
description: Use of analgesic and pain relief medications: acetaminophen (Tylenol), NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen), aspirin for pain, opioid analgesics (morphine, oxycodone, codeine, hydrocodone), and prescription pain medications.
All alcoholic drinks including beer, wine, liquor, and mixed drinks
name: Alcoholic Beverages

description: All non-alcoholic drinks including soft drinks, juices, coffee, tea, and water
name: Non-Alcoholic Beverages

description: All dairy products including milk, cheese, yogurt, cream, and dairy-based desserts
name: Dairy Products

description: All meat and poultry including beef, pork, lamb, chicken, turkey, and processed meats
name: Meat and Poultry

description: All fish and seafood including fresh fish, canned fish, shellfish, and fried seafood
name: Fish and Seafood

description: All fresh, canned, dried, and frozen fruits including citrus fruits, berries, and tropical fruits
name: Fruits

description: All vegetables including leafy greens, root vegetables, cruciferous vegetables, and starchy vegetables
name: Vegetables

description: All grain products including bread, cereals, rice, pasta, and baked goods
name: Grains and Cereals

description: All legumes including dried beans, peas, lentils, and soy products
name: Legumes and Beans

description: All nuts, seeds, and nut butters including peanuts, tree nuts, and seed products
name: Nuts and Seeds

description: All egg products including whole eggs, egg whites, and egg substitutes
name: Eggs

description: All added fats and oils including butter, margarine, cooking oils, and salad dressings
name: Fats and Oils

description: All sweet foods including candy, chocolate, cakes, cookies, pies, and other desserts
name: Sweets and Desserts

description: All snack foods including chips, crackers, popcorn, and other salty snacks
name: Snack Foods

description: All condiments, seasonings, sauces, and flavor enhancers
name: Condiments and Seasonings

description: All types of soups including broth-based, cream-based, and specialty soups
name: Soups

description: Complex dishes combining multiple food groups including casseroles, ethnic dishes, and combination meals
name: Mixed Dishes

description: Specialty and ethnic foods, baby foods, meal replacements, and other unique food items
name: Specialty Foods

description: Variables related to cooking methods, food preparation techniques, and doneness preferences
name: Cooking Methods and Food Preparation

description: Variables related to meal frequency, eating patterns, dining locations, and food behaviors
name: Meal Patterns and Eating Behaviors

description: Variables related to vitamin supplements, fortified foods, and nutritional additives
name: Dietary Supplements and Fortified Foods

description: Variables related to food shopping locations, food access, and food environment
name: Food Shopping and Access

description: Variables related to special diets, dietary restrictions, and overall dietary patterns
name: Dietary Patterns and Restrictions

description: Variables related to infant feeding, breastfeeding, and child nutrition
name: Infant and Child Feeding

description: Variables related to fast food consumption, restaurant eating, and dining out patterns
name: Fast Food and Restaurant Eating

description: General medication use including drug names, dosages, routes, frequencies, and therapeutic classes. Covers all medication types not specifically captured by narrower concepts like antihypertensive_medication or lipid_lowering_medication.
name: Concomitant Medications

description: Medications specifically used to treat high blood pressure including ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, diuretics, and other blood pressure lowering drugs. Includes specific drug names, dosages, and treatment duration.
name: Antihypertensive Medications

description: Medications for heart conditions including digitalis, nitrates, anti-arrhythmics, and other cardiovascular drugs not primarily used for blood pressure control. Includes medications for heart failure, arrhythmias, and coronary artery disease.
name: Cardiac and Cardiovascular Medications

description: Medications for respiratory conditions including bronchodilators, inhaled steroids, asthma medications, COPD treatments, and other pulmonary therapies. Covers inhalers, nebulizers, and oral respiratory medications.
name: Respiratory and Pulmonary Medications

description: All medications related to diabetes management including insulin preparations, oral hypoglycemic agents, glucose monitoring supplies, and diabetes-related treatments. Includes insulin types, dosages, and administration methods.
name: Diabetes and Glucose Management Medications

description: Medications for digestive system conditions including acid reducers, proton pump inhibitors, H2 blockers, anti-nausea drugs, and treatments for GERD, ulcers, and other GI disorders.
name: Gastrointestinal Medications

description: Medications for neurological and psychiatric conditions including antidepressants, antipsychotics, anticonvulsants, anti-anxiety drugs, sleep medications, and treatments for seizures, depression, anxiety, and other mental health conditions.
name: Neurological and Psychiatric Medications

description: Medications for pain management including over-the-counter and prescription pain relievers, anti-inflammatory drugs, opioids, and other analgesics. Covers acute and chronic pain treatments.
name: Pain and Analgesic Medications

description: All antibiotic and antimicrobial medications including specific drug names, dosages, routes of administration, and treatment durations. Covers systemic and topical antibiotics for various infections.
name: Antibiotic and Antimicrobial Medications

description: Hormonal medications including birth control, hormone replacement therapy, fertility treatments, testosterone, estrogen, progesterone, and other reproductive health medications.
name: Hormone and Reproductive Medications

description: Vitamins, dietary supplements, nutritional products, and over-the-counter health products including specific vitamins, minerals, herbal supplements, and nutritional support medications.
name: Vitamins, Supplements, and Nutritional Medications

description: Medications for eye conditions, ear problems, and other sensory organ treatments including eye drops, glaucoma medications, and treatments for vision or hearing disorders.
name: Eye, Ear, and Sensory Medications

description: Medications for bone health, joint conditions, and musculoskeletal disorders including treatments for osteoporosis, arthritis, and bone density medications.
name: Bone, Joint, and Musculoskeletal Medications

description: Medications for kidney function, urinary tract conditions, and renal disorders including diuretics specifically for kidney conditions and treatments for urinary problems.
name: Kidney and Urinary Medications

description: Medications for allergic reactions, immune system disorders, and immunosuppressive treatments including antihistamines, allergy medications, and immune system modulators.
name: Allergy and Immunology Medications

description: Medications for sleep disorders, insomnia, and sedation including sleep aids, hypnotics, and other sleep-related treatments.
name: Sleep and Sedative Medications

description: Medications for cancer treatment including chemotherapy drugs, targeted therapies, immunotherapy, and supportive care medications for cancer patients.
name: Cancer and Chemotherapy Medications

description: Variables related to tracking, managing, and documenting concomitant medications including medication lists, dosing schedules, compliance, verification, and administrative aspects of medication management.
example_variables: 4am_survey: Have you eaten since the previous survey?
name: Concomitant Medication Tracking and Management

description: Miscellaneous medications and treatments that do not fit into other specific categories, including various therapeutic drugs, experimental medications, and unspecified treatments.
name: Other and General Medications

description: Quantitative chest CT scan measurements including lung density in Hounsfield units (HU), percent emphysema, gas trapping, airway wall thickness and area, airway dimensions (Pi10), parametric response mapping (PRM), and lobar segmentation.
name: Lung CT Quantitative Imaging

description: Clinical diagnosis of emphysema, age at diagnosis, doctor confirmation, current status, family history, and medical treatment records
name: Emphysema Diagnosis and Medical History

description: Whole lung emphysema measurements including percent emphysema at various HU thresholds (-910, -950), low attenuation areas, and global lung density statistics
name: Global Emphysema Quantification

description: Emphysema distribution patterns including upper vs lower lobe predominance, apical-basilar ratios, craniocaudal and axial distribution patterns
name: Regional Emphysema Distribution

description: Voxel counts at various Hounsfield Unit thresholds for both lungs combined, including emphysema cutoffs and high/low attenuation measurements
name: Bilateral Lung Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts at various HU thresholds for both lungs core regions (excluding peripheral areas), used for emphysema quantification
name: Bilateral Lung Core Region Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts at various HU thresholds for both lungs peripheral (peel) regions, focusing on outer lung areas for emphysema assessment
name: Bilateral Lung Peripheral Region Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts at various HU thresholds for the left lung, including emphysema measurements and density distribution analysis
name: Left Lung Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts for left lung subdivided into core and peripheral (peel) regions at various HU thresholds for detailed emphysema analysis
name: Left Lung Core and Peripheral Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts for left lung upper, middle, and lower lobes including core and peripheral subdivisions at various HU thresholds
name: Left Lung Lobar Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts at various HU thresholds for the right lung, including emphysema measurements and density distribution analysis
name: Right Lung Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts for right lung subdivided into core and peripheral (peel) regions at various HU thresholds for detailed emphysema analysis
name: Right Lung Core and Peripheral Voxel Counts

description: Voxel counts for right lung upper, middle, and lower lobes including core and peripheral subdivisions at various HU thresholds
name: Right Lung Lobar Voxel Counts

description: Quantitative airway measurements including wall thickness, lumen area, Pi10, wall area percentage, and airway dimensions from various analysis methods
name: Airway Wall and Lumen Measurements

description: Detailed measurements of individual airway branches including trachea, main bronchi, lobar bronchi, and segmental airways with wall and lumen characteristics
name: Specific Airway Branch Measurements

description: Measurements of gas trapping and air trapping including expiratory CT analysis and parametric response mapping for functional small airway disease
name: Gas Trapping and Air Trapping

description: Parametric response mapping measurements comparing inspiratory and expiratory CT scans to identify emphysema and functional small airway disease
name: Parametric Response Mapping (PRM)

description: Quantitative measurements of intraparenchymal blood vessels including volume measurements by vessel size categories
name: Pulmonary Blood Vessel Measurements

description: Detailed lobar and segmental analysis using LOBES and REGIONAL methods including ground glass, reticular patterns, and various low attenuation classifications
name: Lobar Segmentation Analysis

description: Overall lung segmentation measurements including total volume, density statistics, and classification of lung tissue patterns
name: Total Lung Segmentation Analysis

description: CT histogram measurements including percentile values for inspiratory and expiratory scans across different lung regions and lobes
name: CT Histogram Analysis

description: Visual assessment scores including deep learning Fleischner scoring, emphysema patterns, and radiological findings
name: Visual Scoring and Pattern Recognition

description: Spirometry measurements including FEV1, FEV6, FVC, and bronchodilator response testing related to lung function assessment
name: Pulmonary Function Tests

description: Technical CT scan parameters including scanner type, reconstruction settings, slice thickness, and imaging protocols
name: CT Scan Technical Parameters

description: CT measurements of non-pulmonary structures including abdominal fat, muscle, liver, and other anatomical regions captured in chest CT scans
name: Non-Lung CT Measurements

description: Measurements of carotid and other vascular structures including lumen dimensions and wall characteristics
name: Vascular Measurements

description: Study identifiers, visit information, demographics, and clinical metadata not directly related to lung imaging measurements
name: Clinical Demographics and Study Metadata

description: Clinical respiratory conditions and symptoms including pneumonia, dyspnea, sleep apnea, chronic bronchitis, and other lung-related diagnoses
name: Respiratory Clinical Conditions

description: Respiratory support interventions including oxygen therapy, ventilation, CPAP, BiPAP, tracheotomy, and related respiratory care
name: Respiratory Interventions and Support

description: Medical conditions and symptoms not primarily related to the respiratory system including cardiac, neurological, gastrointestinal, and other systemic conditions
name: Non-Respiratory Medical Conditions

description: Developmental milestones, genetic conditions, and congenital abnormalities including MPS-related features and developmental delays
name: Developmental and Genetic Conditions

description: Vision and hearing related conditions including hearing loss, visual impairment, eye surgeries, and sensory aids
name: Sensory and Vision/Hearing Conditions

description: Joint stiffness, contractures, carpal tunnel syndrome, and other musculoskeletal conditions affecting mobility and function
name: Musculoskeletal and Joint Conditions

description: Various therapeutic interventions including physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy, behavioral interventions, and medications
name: Therapeutic Interventions and Medications

description: Surgical procedures with ages, dates, and details including multiple surgery records and intervention histories
name: Surgical Procedures and Medical Interventions

description: Educational interventions, special education services, and academic support including mainstream education and specialized classroom settings
name: Educational Support and Special Services

description: Sleep study measurements from polysomnography (PSG) including apnea-hypopnea index (AHI), oxygen desaturation (SpO2), arousal indices, sleep stage durations (NREM, REM), respiratory event counts, periodic limb movements, and EEG-derived sleep quality parameters.
name: Polysomnography

description: Overall apnea-hypopnea index (AHI) measurements at different desaturation thresholds (0%, 2%, 3%, 4%, 5%) and with/without arousals, including supine vs non-supine positions and REM vs NREM sleep stages
name: Apnea-Hypopnea Indices

description: Central apnea counts, durations, rates, and characteristics by sleep stage (NREM/REM), body position (back/other), and desaturation thresholds, including minimum, maximum, and average event durations
name: Central Apnea Events

description: Obstructive apnea counts, durations, rates, and characteristics by sleep stage (NREM/REM), body position (back/other), and desaturation thresholds, including minimum, maximum, and average event durations
name: Obstructive Apnea Events

description: Hypopnea counts, durations, rates, and characteristics by sleep stage (NREM/REM), body position (back/other), and desaturation thresholds, including minimum, maximum, and average event durations
name: Hypopnea Events

description: SpO2 measurements including baseline, minimum, maximum, and average oxygen saturation levels during sleep, time spent below various saturation thresholds (70%, 75%, 80%, 85%, 90%, 95%), and desaturation event counts and characteristics
name: Oxygen Saturation Measurements

description: Detailed desaturation measurements associated with respiratory events, including average, minimum, and maximum desaturation levels by sleep stage, body position, and desaturation thresholds, with and without arousals
name: Desaturation Event Characteristics

description: Arousal indices, counts, and associated heart rate changes during sleep, including arousal-related heart rate measurements (average, minimum, maximum BPM) by sleep stage and body position
name: Arousal Events and Heart Rate

description: Sleep stage durations, percentages, transitions, and sleep efficiency measurements including REM, NREM, stage 1, stage 2, stage 3-4 sleep times and transitions between stages
name: Sleep Architecture and Staging

description: Clinical diagnosis of sleep apnea, age at diagnosis, family history, treatment methods (CPAP, surgery), and sleep apnea risk assessments
name: Sleep Apnea Diagnosis and Treatment

description: Subjective sleep symptoms including snoring, witnessed apneas, daytime sleepiness, sleep quality ratings, and sleep-related questionnaire responses
name: Sleep Symptoms and Quality

description: Polysomnography signal quality assessments, electrode placements, technical issues, artifact detection, and data reliability measures for all recorded channels
name: PSG Signal Quality and Technical

description: Polysomnography equipment identifiers, technician information, study dates, times, monitor setup, and administrative details related to PSG data collection
name: PSG Equipment and Setup

description: Medications, drugs, and substances that may affect sleep including sleep medications, cardiovascular drugs, respiratory medications, alcohol, caffeine, and tobacco use before sleep studies
name: Medications and Substances

description: General health questionnaires, quality of life measures, physical functioning assessments, and health-related survey responses not specifically related to sleep
name: Health and Quality of Life Questionnaires

description: Medical history including cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological conditions, and other health conditions that may be related to or affect sleep disorders
name: Medical History and Comorbidities

description: Sleep timing patterns, bedtime routines, sleep duration, wake times, and sleep habits on weekdays vs weekends
name: Sleep Habits and Timing

description: Brain imaging measurements, biomarkers, and laboratory values including parahippocampal measurements, amyloid and tau markers, and other biological indicators
name: Neuroimaging and Biomarkers

description: Movement disorder assessments, tremor measurements, rigidity evaluations, and other neurological examination findings
name: Movement and Neurological Assessments

description: Administrative variables related to study conduct, participant recruitment, follow-up contacts, and data collection procedures
name: Study Administration and Follow-up

description: Standardized depression and anxiety screening instruments including CES-D (Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression), PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire), GAD-7, Beck Depression Inventory, SF-36 mental health subscales, and related item-level and summary scores.
name: Depression and Anxiety Screening

description: Individual item-level responses from the Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression Scale (CES-D), including specific symptoms like feeling depressed, bothered by things, crying spells, loneliness, fearfulness, sleep problems, and other mood-related items assessed over the past week.
name: CES-D Individual Items

description: Total scores, composite measures, and summary statistics from the Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression Scale (CES-D), including raw totals, standardized scores, and administrative flags.
name: CES-D Total and Summary Scores

description: Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) depression scores and Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7) anxiety scores, including total scores across multiple time points and severity categories.
name: PHQ-9 and GAD-7 Scores

description: Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) individual items, subscale scores, and summary measures for both anxiety and depression components.
name: HADS Items and Scores

description: Short Form-36 (SF-36) mental health subscale items, raw scores, standardized scores, and mental component summary measures assessing psychological well-being and emotional functioning.
name: SF-36 Mental Health Measures

description: Short Form-12 (SF-12) items specifically related to feeling depressed, downhearted, and emotional problems affecting work and daily activities.
name: SF-12 Depression and Emotional Items

description: Questions about how emotional problems (such as feeling depressed or anxious) have limited work performance, daily activities, or caused accomplishing less than desired.
name: Emotional Problems Affecting Work/Activities

description: Child Behavior Checklist (CBCL) anxious-depressed and withdrawn-depressed subscales, including raw scores, T-scores, and significance levels for parent-reported child behavior problems.
name: Child Behavior Checklist Depression/Anxiety

description: Structured clinical interview questions about major depressive episodes, including onset, duration, severity, recurrence, and diagnostic criteria from instruments like DIGS (Diagnostic Interview for Genetic Studies).
name: Clinical Depression Episodes and History

description: Depression symptoms occurring in the context of substance use, withdrawal, or substance-induced psychological problems, including alcohol, drugs, tobacco, and various substance classes.
name: Substance Use Related Depression

description: Family history and genetic risk factors for depression, including depression in parents, siblings, children, and grandparents.
name: Family History of Depression

description: Psychotic symptoms occurring during depressive or manic episodes, mood-congruent and mood-incongruent psychotic features, and mixed affective states.
name: Psychotic Symptoms with Mood Episodes

description: Parent-reported child depression symptoms, behavioral history, age of onset, and current/past depressed mood in children and adolescents.
name: Child Depression and Behavioral History

description: Miscellaneous depression-related items including single-item measures, trait assessments, POMS scales, SCL-90 depression, and context-specific depression questions.
name: Other Depression and Mood Measures

description: Patient Health Questionnaire medical history and clinical assessment forms, primarily focused on cardiac and medical conditions rather than depression screening.
name: PHQ Medical History Forms

description: Diagnostic psychiatric interview data from instruments like DIGS, CIDI, SCID, MINI, and NESARC/AUDADIS. Covers substance use disorders, mood disorders (bipolar, major depression), anxiety disorders, psychotic disorders, PTSD, ADHD, eating disorders, and personality disorders.
name: Structured Psychiatric Interview

description: Variables related to alcohol, drug, tobacco, and other substance use disorders including abuse, dependence, withdrawal symptoms, treatment history, and substance-related problems. Covers alcohol use disorder, cannabis abuse/dependence, cocaine abuse/dependence, opioid use, sedative use, stimulant use, tobacco/nicotine dependence, and other substance use patterns.
name: Substance Use Disorders

description: Variables related to bipolar disorder, major depression, mania, hypomania, dysthymia, cyclothymia, and other mood episodes. Includes symptoms, severity, duration, onset age, treatment, hospitalization, and functional impairment related to mood disorders.
name: Mood Disorders

description: Variables related to schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder, and psychotic symptoms including delusions, hallucinations, thought disorders, disorganized behavior, negative symptoms, and psychotic episodes. Covers onset, duration, severity, and functional impact of psychotic symptoms.
name: Psychotic Disorders and Symptoms

description: Variables related to panic disorder, agoraphobia, social phobia, specific phobias, generalized anxiety disorder, and anxiety symptoms. Includes panic attacks, avoidance behaviors, social anxiety, phobic fears, and anxiety-related functional impairment.
name: Anxiety Disorders

description: Variables related to obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), obsessions, compulsions, repetitive behaviors, and related symptoms. Includes checking behaviors, counting, ordering, cleaning rituals, and other compulsive behaviors.
name: Obsessive-Compulsive and Related Disorders

description: Variables related to attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), attention problems, hyperactivity, impulsivity, and related behavioral symptoms. Includes diagnostic assessments, symptom severity, and functional impairment.
name: ADHD and Attention Disorders

description: Variables related to anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, binge eating disorder, and other eating disorders. Includes weight concerns, body image, eating behaviors, compensatory behaviors, and eating disorder treatment.
name: Eating Disorders

description: Variables related to post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), trauma exposure, trauma symptoms, and stress-related disorders. Includes trauma history, PTSD symptoms, and trauma-related functional impairment.
name: Trauma and PTSD

description: Variables related to conduct disorder, antisocial personality disorder, and antisocial behaviors. Includes aggression, rule-breaking, criminal behavior, and antisocial traits.
name: Conduct and Antisocial Disorders

description: Variables related to pathological gambling, gambling behaviors, and gambling-related problems. Includes gambling frequency, gambling-related financial and social problems.
name: Gambling Disorders

description: Variables related to suicidal ideation, suicide attempts, self-harm behaviors, and suicidal risk factors. Includes method, lethality, intent, and circumstances of suicide attempts.
name: Suicide and Self-Harm

description: Variables related to personality traits, interpersonal functioning, social relationships, and personality disorder symptoms. Includes paranoid traits, social isolation, interpersonal difficulties, and personality assessment.
name: Personality Traits and Disorders

description: Variables related to psychiatric medications, treatment history, hospitalization, and therapeutic interventions. Includes antidepressants, antipsychotics, mood stabilizers, anxiolytics, and treatment response.
name: Medications and Treatment

description: Variables related to medical history, physical health conditions, neurological problems, and medical factors that may impact psychiatric symptoms. Includes head injuries, seizures, thyroid disorders, and other medical conditions.
name: Medical History and Conditions

description: Variables related to physical symptoms, somatization, pain complaints, gastrointestinal symptoms, neurological symptoms, and medically unexplained symptoms. Includes pain syndromes and conversion symptoms.
name: Somatic Symptoms and Somatization

description: Variables related to demographic information, family background, education, employment, military service, and social history. Includes age, ethnicity, marital status, education level, and occupational history.
name: Demographics and Background

description: Variables related to interview procedures, assessment measures, cognitive testing, reliability ratings, and clinical observations during psychiatric interviews. Includes mental status exam components and interviewer ratings.
name: Interview Assessment and Measures

description: Variables related to family psychiatric history, genetic factors, and familial patterns of mental illness. Includes family history of bipolar disorder, depression, ADHD, OCD, and other psychiatric conditions in relatives.
name: Family History

description: Variables containing diagnostic codes, classification systems, confidence ratings, and formal diagnostic assignments. Includes DSM codes, ICD codes, RDC criteria, and diagnostic confidence measures.
name: Diagnostic Codes and Classifications

description: Cancer occurrence, type, histology, staging, and screening. Includes self-reported cancer history by site, cancer registry data (ICD-O morphology, behavior, grade, Gleason score), mammography, PSA testing, Pap smear, and colonoscopy screening.
name: Cancer Diagnosis and Screening

description: Variables indicating whether a subject has been diagnosed with cancer, case-control status, affection status, and general cancer presence indicators. Includes binary yes/no cancer variables and case/control classifications.
name: Cancer Occurrence and Status

description: Variables capturing the age at which various cancers were first diagnosed, including specific cancer types and general cancer diagnosis ages. Covers both self-reported and clinically confirmed diagnosis ages.
name: Age at Cancer Diagnosis

description: Variables specifying the anatomical site, organ, or body location where cancer occurred, as well as specific cancer types and histological classifications. Includes primary site codes and detailed cancer type categorizations.
name: Cancer Site and Type

description: Variables describing tumor properties including histology, grade, stage, size, morphology, differentiation, and pathological features. Covers tumor classification systems, staging criteria, and detailed pathological assessments.
name: Tumor Characteristics and Pathology

description: Variables related to cancer treatments including chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surgery, hormone therapy, immunotherapy, and other therapeutic interventions. Covers treatment history, current treatments, and treatment responses.
name: Cancer Treatment and Therapy

description: Variables related to cancer screening tests, preventive measures, and early detection activities. Includes mammograms, Pap smears, PSA tests, colonoscopies, and other screening procedures.
name: Cancer Screening and Prevention

description: Variables documenting cancer history in family members including parents, siblings, children, and other relatives. Covers specific cancer types in family members and family cancer patterns.
name: Family History of Cancer

description: Variables representing cancer-related biomarkers, molecular markers, genetic alterations, and protein expressions. Includes tumor markers, gene mutations, protein levels, and molecular profiling data.
name: Biomarkers and Molecular Markers

description: Variables related to cancer outcomes, survival times, progression, recurrence, mortality, and follow-up data. Includes event-free survival, disease progression, and death from cancer.
name: Cancer Outcomes and Survival

description: Variables containing standardized cancer classification codes, ICD-O morphology codes, SEER codes, and other registry-based cancer classification systems used for cancer surveillance and research.
name: Cancer Registry and Classification Codes

description: Variables related to cancer risk factors, environmental exposures, lifestyle factors, and conditions that may predispose to cancer development. Includes sun exposure, occupational hazards, and other risk-associated factors.
name: Cancer Risk Factors and Exposures

description: Variables from clinical records, medical history, physical examinations, and healthcare encounters related to cancer diagnosis and management. Includes clinical assessments and medical documentation.
name: Clinical Data and Medical Records

description: Non-cardiovascular, non-cancer disease diagnoses and medical history. Includes thyroid disease, arthritis (osteo, rheumatoid, gout), kidney disease, liver disease, gallbladder disease, seizure disorders, Parkinson's disease, asthma/COPD diagnosis, diabetes diagnosis, and other self-reported chronic conditions.
name: Disease Diagnosis History

description: Diagnoses and history of respiratory conditions including asthma, COPD, chronic bronchitis, emphysema, pulmonary fibrosis, interstitial lung disease, and other chronic lung conditions. Includes physician-diagnosed respiratory diseases and related symptoms.
name: Respiratory and Lung Disease

description: Chronic kidney disease, end-stage renal disease, kidney stones, polycystic kidney disease, and other renal conditions. Includes case-control status for kidney disease studies and related phenotypes.
name: Kidney and Renal Disease

description: Liver disease including hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver disease, autoimmune liver disease, alcoholic liver disease, and other chronic liver conditions. Includes liver disease staging and related clinical assessments.
name: Liver Disease

description: Gallbladder disease, gallstones, and related biliary conditions. Includes gallbladder surgery and gallbladder-related diagnostic impressions.
name: Gallbladder Disease

description: Thyroid disorders including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, thyroid surgery, thyroid medication use, and other thyroid-related conditions.
name: Thyroid Disease

description: Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, dementia, tremor, and other neurological conditions. Includes UPDRS scores, family history of neurological diseases, and related movement disorder assessments.
name: Neurological and Movement Disorders

description: Seizure disorders, epilepsy, and related neurological conditions requiring chronic medication or treatment. Includes seizure history and diagnostic assessments.
name: Seizure and Epilepsy Disorders

description: Type 1 and type 2 diabetes, diabetes diagnosis codes, age at diagnosis, and related metabolic conditions. Includes diabetes case-control status and diagnostic criteria.
name: Diabetes and Metabolic Disorders

description: Osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, gout, and other joint diseases. Includes degenerative joint disease and arthritis-related conditions.
name: Arthritis and Joint Disorders

description: Autoimmune diseases including lupus, multiple sclerosis, psoriasis, celiac disease, and other autoimmune conditions. Includes diagnosis ages and related autoimmune illnesses.
name: Autoimmune Disorders

description: Gastrointestinal diseases including inflammatory bowel disease, ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease, diverticular disease, irritable bowel syndrome, and other GI conditions.
name: Gastrointestinal Disorders

description: Genitourinary conditions including prostate disease, gynecological conditions, urinary tract disease, and reproductive health issues.
name: Genitourinary Disorders

description: Blood disorders, bleeding disorders, and hematologic diseases. Includes diagnosis ages and related blood conditions.
name: Hematologic Disorders

description: Infectious diseases including hepatitis, HIV/AIDS, and other infectious conditions. Includes diagnosis ages and related infectious disease history.
name: Infectious Diseases

description: Mental health conditions including ADHD, autism spectrum disorders, depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, and other psychiatric diagnoses. Includes family history of psychiatric problems.
name: Psychiatric and Mental Health Disorders

description: Autism spectrum disorders and developmental conditions including ADI-R algorithm scores, developmental milestones, and behavioral assessments related to autism diagnosis.
name: Autism and Developmental Disorders

description: Cardiovascular conditions mentioned in disease diagnosis context including cardiac disease history in family members and hypertension status.
name: Cardiovascular-Related Conditions

description: Cancer diagnoses, cancer sites, and cancer-related conditions including breast cancer history and various cancer types.
name: Cancer-Related Conditions

description: Miscellaneous chronic conditions and diseases not fitting into other categories, including sleep disorders, hearing problems, allergies, and other specified medical conditions.
name: Other Chronic Conditions

description: ICD codes, cause of death codes, diagnostic codes, and medical coding variables used for classification and record-keeping purposes.
name: Medical Coding and Death Records

description: Eye examination findings and ophthalmologic measurements including visual acuity, intraocular pressure, slit-lamp findings, retinal exam (macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy staging, drusen), cataract grading, glaucoma evaluation, corneal measurements, and fundus photography.
name: Ophthalmology Assessment

description: Visual acuity measurements including Snellen charts, letter counting tests, corrected and uncorrected vision, visual acuity scores, and related testing procedures at various distances and conditions.
name: Visual Acuity Testing

description: Intraocular pressure readings, tonometry measurements, pressure monitoring over time, and pressure-related assessments for glaucoma evaluation.
name: Intraocular Pressure Measurements

description: Spherical and cylindrical refractive measurements, axis measurements, corrective lens prescriptions, and subjective refraction testing for distance vision.
name: Refractive Error and Lens Correction

description: Diabetic retinopathy grading, ETDRS levels, microaneurysms, hemorrhages, hard exudates, soft exudates, neovascularization, macular edema, and diabetic retinopathy progression monitoring.
name: Diabetic Retinopathy Assessment

description: Age-related macular degeneration assessment including drusen size, type, area, confluence, geographic atrophy, pigmentary changes, neovascular AMD, and AREDS grading.
name: Age-Related Macular Degeneration and Drusen

description: Cataract grading, lens opacity measurements including nuclear, cortical, and posterior subcapsular cataracts, lens examination findings, and cataract surgery history.
name: Cataract and Lens Assessment

description: Glaucoma diagnosis, cup-to-disc ratio measurements, optic disc evaluation, glaucomatous optic neuropathy, visual field defects, and glaucoma treatment history.
name: Glaucoma and Optic Disc Assessment

description: Retinal vascular abnormalities including arteriovenous nicking, focal narrowing, arterial and venous occlusions, retinal vessel measurements, and hypertensive retinopathy.
name: Retinal Vascular Disease

description: Corneal examination including arcus senilis, corneal thickness, corneal pathology, anterior chamber depth, angle assessment, iris examination, and pupil reactions.
name: Corneal and Anterior Segment Examination

description: Fundus photography procedures, image quality assessment, photo grading, field completeness, focus quality, and photographic documentation of retinal findings.
name: Fundus Photography and Image Quality

description: Laser photocoagulation, focal and scatter laser treatment, vitrectomy, retinal detachment surgery, and other ocular surgical procedures.
name: Laser Treatment and Ocular Surgery

description: Miscellaneous retinal conditions including asteroid hyalosis, chorioretinal scars, macular holes, nevus, histoplasmosis, angioid streaks, and other retinal abnormalities.
name: Other Retinal Pathology

description: Slit lamp biomicroscopy findings including anterior segment evaluation, lens examination, vitreous assessment, and detailed ocular structure examination.
name: Slit Lamp Examination

description: Color vision assessment, visual field testing, perimetry results, and specialized visual function measurements.
name: Color Vision and Visual Field Testing

description: Eye drops, ocular medications including steroids, glaucoma medications, anti-VEGF treatments, and other topical or injectable ocular therapies.
name: Ocular Medications and Treatments

description: Axial length measurements, ocular biometry, and dimensional assessments of the eye for refractive and surgical planning purposes.
name: Ocular Biometry and Axial Measurements

description: Retinopathy of prematurity assessment, ROP staging, severity grading, and related findings in premature infants.
name: Retinopathy of Prematurity

description: Genetic eye diseases, hereditary retinal conditions, corneal dystrophies, and familial eye disease patterns identified by OMIM codes and genetic classifications.
name: Genetic and Hereditary Eye Conditions

description: Study identifiers, visit numbers, data collection dates, form completion, consent documentation, and administrative variables for research data management.
name: Study Administration and Data Management

description: Neurological manifestations affecting eye movement, including nystagmus, saccadic abnormalities, tremors, dystonia, and other movement disorders that impact ocular function.
name: Neurological and Movement Disorders

description: Systemic diseases and medical conditions that may affect ocular health, including diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and other relevant medical history.
name: Systemic Comorbidities and Medical History

description: Reproductive health history including pregnancy count, outcomes, and complications (pre-eclampsia, gestational diabetes, miscarriage), menstrual history, age at menarche/menopause, contraception use, fertility/infertility, hysterectomy, hormone replacement therapy, and breastfeeding.
name: Pregnancy and Reproductive Health

description: Variables related to menstrual cycle characteristics including age at menarche, menstrual period regularity, cycle duration, menstrual bleeding patterns, and current menstrual status. Includes variables about first menstrual period, cycle phase, and menstrual product use.
name: Menstrual Cycle and Menarche

description: Variables covering menopause status, age at menopause, type of menopause (natural vs surgical), and hormone replacement therapy use including estrogen, progesterone, and other menopausal treatments. Includes menopausal symptoms and related medications.
name: Menopause and Hormone Replacement Therapy

description: Variables related to contraceptive use including birth control pills, hormonal contraceptives, barrier methods, IUDs, sterilization procedures, and other forms of contraception. Includes duration of use, brand names, and reasons for contraceptive choices.
name: Contraception and Birth Control

description: Variables documenting total number of pregnancies (gravidity), pregnancy history, current pregnancy status, and basic pregnancy-related demographics. Includes variables about ever being pregnant and pregnancy counts over time.
name: Pregnancy Count and History

description: Variables related to pregnancy outcomes including live births, stillbirths, miscarriages, abortions, gestational age at delivery, birth weight, delivery method, and neonatal outcomes. Includes specific pregnancy outcome details and delivery complications.
name: Pregnancy Outcomes and Delivery

description: Variables specifically measuring gestational age at various time points including at birth, at sample collection, at specific procedures, and estimated gestational age. Includes both weeks and days measurements of pregnancy duration.
name: Gestational Age and Timing

description: Variables documenting pregnancy complications including preeclampsia, eclampsia, gestational diabetes, gestational hypertension, HELLP syndrome, placental problems, bleeding, and other maternal complications during pregnancy.
name: Pregnancy Complications

description: Variables related to fertility issues, infertility treatments, assisted reproductive technologies (IVF, IUI, ICSI), fertility medications, and difficulties conceiving. Includes both male and female fertility factors and treatments.
name: Fertility and Infertility

description: Variables related to breastfeeding practices including duration of breastfeeding, exclusive breastfeeding, breastfeeding difficulties, medications while breastfeeding, and lactation support. Includes breastfeeding history for multiple children.
name: Breastfeeding and Lactation

description: Variables related to hysterectomy procedures, ovarian removal, and other gynecologic surgeries including age at surgery, type of surgery, reasons for surgery, and surgical menopause. Includes both partial and complete procedures.
name: Hysterectomy and Gynecologic Surgery

description: Variables related to prenatal care including prenatal testing, amniocentesis, ultrasounds, genetic screening, prenatal vitamins, and other prenatal medical care and monitoring during pregnancy.
name: Prenatal Care and Testing

description: Variables documenting maternal exposures during pregnancy including alcohol consumption, smoking, drug use, environmental exposures, occupational exposures, and other potentially harmful substances or conditions during pregnancy.
name: Maternal Exposures During Pregnancy

description: Variables capturing specific dates and timing related to pregnancies including pregnancy start dates, end dates, years of pregnancies, timing relative to other events, and chronological pregnancy information.
name: Pregnancy Timing and Dates

description: Variables specifically related to timing of blood draws and sample collection in relation to pregnancy, menstrual cycle, and other reproductive events. Includes time-specific sample collection data.
name: Blood Draw and Sample Timing

description: High-throughput protein measurements from platforms like SOMAscan (aptamer-based) and Olink (proximity extension assay). Measures hundreds to thousands of plasma proteins including cytokines, growth factors, enzymes, receptors, and structural proteins.
name: Proteomics Panel

description: Inflammatory signaling molecules including interleukins, chemokines (CCL, CXCL, CX3CL), tumor necrosis factors, interferons, and other immune mediators that regulate inflammation, immune responses, and cell communication.
name: Cytokines and Chemokines

description: Proteins that stimulate cellular growth, proliferation, differentiation, and tissue development including fibroblast growth factors (FGF), bone morphogenetic proteins (BMP), vascular endothelial growth factors (VEGF), platelet-derived growth factors (PDGF), and related signaling molecules.
name: Growth Factors and Morphogens

description: Components of the complement cascade (C1-C9, factors B, D, H, I) and blood coagulation system including clotting factors, fibrinogen, plasminogen activators, and anticoagulant proteins that regulate hemostasis and immune defense.
name: Complement System and Coagulation Factors

description: Proteins involved in cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions including cadherins, integrins, selectins, immunoglobulin superfamily members, extracellular matrix components like collagens, laminins, fibronectin, and proteoglycans.
name: Cell Adhesion and Extracellular Matrix

description: Enzymes that cleave proteins and their regulatory inhibitors including matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs), cathepsins, kallikreins, serine proteases, cysteine proteases, and their tissue inhibitors (TIMPs) and serpins.
name: Proteases and Protease Inhibitors

description: Antibodies (IgA, IgD, IgG, IgM), immune receptors including Fc receptors, complement receptors, killer cell receptors, and other immunoglobulin superfamily members involved in adaptive and innate immunity.
name: Immunoglobulins and Immune Receptors

description: Endocrine hormones, neuropeptides, and their binding proteins including insulin, growth hormone, thyroid hormones, steroid hormones, gastrointestinal peptides, and neurohormones that regulate metabolism, growth, and physiological processes.
name: Hormones and Neuropeptides

description: Apolipoproteins, lipid transport proteins, lipid-metabolizing enzymes, and sterol-related proteins involved in cholesterol and lipid homeostasis including HDL, LDL components and lipid transfer proteins.
name: Lipoproteins and Lipid Metabolism

description: Enzymes involved in cellular metabolism including glycolysis, TCA cycle, oxidative phosphorylation, amino acid metabolism, and their cofactors. Also includes metabolic regulatory proteins and energy metabolism markers.
name: Metabolic Enzymes and Cofactors

description: Protein kinases, phosphatases, and related signaling enzymes including tyrosine kinases, serine/threonine kinases, phosphatases, and their regulatory proteins involved in cellular signaling cascades.
name: Kinases and Phosphatases

description: Transcription factors, chromatin remodeling proteins, histones, and other nuclear proteins involved in gene expression regulation, DNA binding, and epigenetic modifications.
name: Transcription Factors and Gene Regulation

description: Proteins regulating programmed cell death, cell cycle progression, DNA repair, and cellular stress responses including caspases, Bcl family proteins, p53 pathway components, and DNA damage response proteins.
name: Apoptosis and Cell Cycle Regulation

description: Proteins involved in molecular transport, binding, and trafficking including albumin, transferrin, binding globulins, carrier proteins, and membrane transporters that facilitate movement of molecules across cellular compartments.
name: Transport and Binding Proteins

description: Membrane receptors, co-receptors, and signaling molecules including growth factor receptors, cytokine receptors, G-protein coupled receptors, and other cell surface proteins involved in signal transduction.
name: Cell Surface Receptors and Signaling

description: Proteins forming cellular structure and cytoskeleton including actins, myosins, tubulins, intermediate filaments, motor proteins, and associated regulatory proteins that maintain cell shape and enable movement.
name: Structural and Cytoskeletal Proteins

description: Ribosomal proteins, translation factors, tRNA synthetases, and other components of the protein synthesis machinery including elongation factors, initiation factors, and ribosome-associated proteins.
name: Ribosomal and Translation Machinery

description: Heat shock proteins, molecular chaperones, protein folding assistants, and cellular stress response proteins that maintain protein homeostasis and respond to environmental stresses.
name: Chaperones and Stress Response

description: Proteins specific to nervous system function, neurodegeneration, and brain-related pathologies including neurofilaments, neural cell adhesion molecules, neurotransmitter-related proteins, and CSF-specific markers.
name: Neurological and CNS Biomarkers

description: Non-protein variables including sample identifiers, collection information, quality control metrics, demographic data, clinical variables, and technical parameters related to sample processing and analysis.
name: Sample and Study Metadata

description: Small molecule metabolites, lipids, sterols, and other non-protein bioactive compounds measured in biological samples including bile acids, fatty acids, amino acids, and their derivatives.
name: Metabolomics and Small Molecules

description: UniProt accession numbers and protein identifiers used for cross-referencing and database linking of protein measurements in proteomics datasets.
name: UniProt Protein Identifiers

description: Internal proteomics database identifiers, protein accession numbers, and technical identifiers used for protein quantification and database management in large-scale proteomics studies.
name: Proteomics Database Identifiers

description: Protein measurements from Olink proximity extension assay panels including cardiovascular, inflammation, organ damage, and cardiometabolic panels with their specific protein targets and quality control metrics.
name: Olink Protein Panel Measurements

description: Brain MRI measurements including FreeSurfer-derived cortical thickness, surface area, and gray matter volume by region (Destrieux/Desikan atlas parcellations), subcortical volumes (hippocampus, amygdala, caudate, putamen, thalamus), white matter hyperintensities, total intracranial volume, and diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) metrics.
name: Brain Neuroimaging

description: Cortical thickness measurements from FreeSurfer analysis, including regional thickness values from Desikan-Killiany and Destrieux atlases for both hemispheres, as well as whole-brain cortical thickness measures.
name: Cortical Thickness

description: Surface area measurements of cortical regions from FreeSurfer analysis using Desikan-Killiany and Destrieux atlases, including regional surface areas for both left and right hemispheres.
name: Cortical Surface Area

description: Gray matter volume measurements of cortical regions from FreeSurfer analysis, including regional volumes from Desikan-Killiany and Destrieux atlases, total cortical volumes, and hemisphere-specific cortical volumes.
name: Cortical Gray Matter Volume

description: Volume measurements of subcortical brain structures including hippocampus, amygdala, caudate, putamen, pallidum, thalamus, nucleus accumbens, and other deep gray matter structures from FreeSurfer segmentation.
name: Subcortical Structure Volumes

description: Volume measurements of ventricular system and cerebrospinal fluid spaces, including lateral ventricles, third ventricle, fourth ventricle, fifth ventricle, and total CSF volume.
name: Ventricular and CSF Volumes

description: Volume measurements of white matter regions including cerebral white matter, cerebellar white matter, cortical white matter, and white matter parcellations from FreeSurfer analysis.
name: White Matter Volumes

description: Volume measurements of cerebellar structures including cerebellar cortex and cerebellar white matter for both hemispheres, as well as total cerebellar volumes.
name: Cerebellar Volumes

description: Volume measurements of brainstem structures including brain stem volume and related brainstem regions.
name: Brainstem Volumes

description: Volume and morphometric measurements of corpus callosum including total corpus callosum volume and its subdivisions (genu, body, splenium, anterior, central, posterior segments).
name: Corpus Callosum

description: Global brain volume measurements including total intracranial volume, estimated total intracranial volume, brain segmentation volume, supratentorial volume, and total gray matter volume.
name: Total Brain Volumes

description: Volume measurements of white matter hyperintensities, hypointensities, and lesions detected on MRI including FLAIR and DSE sequences, as well as lesion counts and characteristics.
name: White Matter Hyperintensities and Lesions

description: Amyloid PET tracer uptake measurements across brain regions, including both raw amyloid values and amyloid-corrected measurements for various cortical and subcortical structures.
name: Amyloid PET Measurements

description: Tau PET tracer uptake measurements across brain regions, including both raw tau values and tau-corrected measurements for various cortical and subcortical structures.
name: Tau PET Measurements

description: FDG PET glucose metabolism measurements across brain regions including mean and standard deviation values for various cortical, subcortical, and cerebellar regions.
name: FDG PET Measurements

description: Functional MRI activation measurements including neural activation in specific brain regions during tasks, particularly food-related activation in amygdala, hypothalamus, and lateral hypothalamus.
name: Functional MRI Activation

description: Diffusion tensor imaging measurements including fractional anisotropy, mean diffusivity, apparent diffusion coefficient, and white matter tract measurements such as cingulum.
name: DTI and Diffusion Measurements

description: Magnetic resonance spectroscopy measurements including NAA/Creatine ratios and other metabolite measurements from brain tissue.
name: MR Spectroscopy

description: Cortical morphometric measurements including mean curvature, thickness standard deviation, and other surface-based morphometric measures from FreeSurfer analysis.
name: Cortical Curvature and Morphometry

description: Volume measurements of other brain structures including optic chiasm, vessels, and various anatomical regions not covered in other categories.
name: Other Brain Structures

description: Pulmonary function test parameters beyond basic spirometry (FEV1, FVC). Includes forced expiratory flow at 25%, 50%, 75% of FVC (FEF25, FEF50, FEF75), peak expiratory flow (PEF), maximum mid-expiratory flow rate (MMEF/FEF25-75), diffusing capacity (DLCO), total lung capacity (TLC), residual volume (RV), and post-bronchodilator responses.
name: Detailed Pulmonary Function Testing

description: Forced expiratory flow measurements at specific percentages of FVC including FEF25, FEF50, FEF75, FEF25-75 (MMEF), and peak expiratory flow (PEF). Includes pre- and post-bronchodilator values, predicted values, percent predicted, quality indicators, and bronchodilator response calculations.
name: Spirometry Flow Rates

description: Basic spirometry measurements including forced vital capacity (FVC), forced expiratory volume in 1 second (FEV1), FEV1/FVC ratio, and forced expiratory volume at 3 and 6 seconds. Includes pre- and post-bronchodilator values, predicted values, percent predicted, lower limits of normal, and bronchodilator response calculations.
name: Spirometry Volumes and Ratios

description: Static lung volume measurements including total lung capacity (TLC), residual volume (RV), inspiratory capacity (IC), slow vital capacity (SVC), and functional residual capacity (FRC). Includes ratios like RV/TLC and IC/TLC, predicted values, and plethysmography measurements.
name: Lung Volumes and Capacities

description: Diffusing capacity of the lungs for carbon monoxide measurements including raw DLCO values, DLCO adjusted for hemoglobin and altitude, DLCO/VA ratios, predicted values, z-scores, and quality indicators. Includes multiple trial measurements and pass/fail grades.
name: Diffusion Capacity (DLCO)

description: Technical aspects of spirometry testing including quality grades, acceptability indicators, reproducibility measures, number of acceptable maneuvers, extrapolated volumes, forced expiratory time, calibration factors, temperature corrections, and reasons for test completion or failure.
name: Spirometry Quality and Technical Parameters

description: Clinical respiratory conditions and symptoms including asthma, COPD, chronic bronchitis, emphysema, bronchiectasis, sleep apnea, wheezing, cough, phlegm production, shortness of breath, and related diagnostic classifications and severity scores.
name: Respiratory Symptoms and Diagnoses
