description: Medications used for respiratory conditions including bronchodilators (short and long-acting), inhaled corticosteroids, combination therapies, nebulized treatments, and their effectiveness. Includes specific drug names, delivery methods, and relief assessments.
name: Respiratory Medications and Treatments

description: Exercise testing parameters, functional capacity assessments, and physical performance measures including cardiopulmonary exercise test results, six-minute walk test data, oxygen saturation during exercise, and related physiological responses.
name: Exercise and Functional Capacity

description: Targeted and untargeted metabolomic and lipidomic profiling. Includes ceramides, sphingomyelins, phosphatidylcholines (PC), phosphatidylethanolamines (PE), diacylglycerols (DG), triacylglycerols (TAG/TG), acylcarnitines, cholesterol esters (CE), lysophospholipids, and other plasma metabolites measured by mass spectrometry.
name: Metabolomics and Lipidomics

description: Acylcarnitines including short-, medium-, and long-chain acylcarnitines, hydroxylated acylcarnitines, and dicarboxylacylcarnitines. These are fatty acid oxidation intermediates measured by mass spectrometry.
name: Acylcarnitines

description: Phosphatidylcholines including diacyl-PC, alkyl-PC (ether-linked), and alkenyl-PC (plasmalogen) species. Identified by carbon chain length and degree of unsaturation (e.g., PC(32:1), PC(34:2)).
name: Phosphatidylcholines (PC)

description: Lysophosphatidylcholines including acyl-LPC, alkyl-LPC (ether-linked), and alkenyl-LPC (plasmalogen) species. These are PC species with one fatty acid chain removed.
name: Lysophosphatidylcholines (LPC)

description: Phosphatidylethanolamines including diacyl-PE, alkyl-PE (ether-linked), and alkenyl-PE (plasmalogen) species. PE lipids are major membrane phospholipids.
name: Phosphatidylethanolamines (PE)

description: Lysophosphatidylethanolamines including acyl-LPE and alkenyl-LPE (plasmalogen) species. These are PE species with one fatty acid chain removed.
name: Lysophosphatidylethanolamines (LPE)

description: Sphingomyelins with various fatty acid chain lengths and degrees of unsaturation. These are sphingolipids containing phosphocholine head groups.
name: Sphingomyelins (SM)

description: Ceramides, dihydroceramides, deoxyceramides, hexosylceramides, and gangliosides. These are sphingolipids with various fatty acid chain lengths and sphingoid base structures.
name: Ceramides and Related Sphingolipids

description: Cholesteryl esters with various fatty acid chain lengths and degrees of unsaturation. Includes oxidized cholesterol esters and modified cholesterol esters.
name: Cholesterol Esters (CE)

description: Diacylglycerols with various fatty acid combinations. These are neutral lipids that serve as intermediates in lipid metabolism and signaling molecules.
name: Diacylglycerols (DAG)

description: Triacylglycerols with various fatty acid combinations and carbon chain lengths. These are the major storage form of lipids and include both regular and ether-linked species.
name: Triacylglycerols (TAG/TG)

description: Phosphatidylserines (PS), phosphatidylinositols (PI), phosphatidylglycerols (PG), phosphatidic acids (PA), and lysophosphatidylinositols (LPI). These are membrane phospholipids with various head groups.
name: Other Phospholipids

description: Free fatty acids including saturated, monounsaturated, and polyunsaturated fatty acids. These are unesterified fatty acids measured in plasma.
name: Free Fatty Acids

description: Primary and secondary bile acids including conjugated and unconjugated forms. These include cholate, deoxycholate, chenodeoxycholate, and their glycine and taurine conjugates.
name: Bile Acids

description: Proteinogenic and non-proteinogenic amino acids, amino acid derivatives, and related nitrogen-containing metabolites including methylated, acetylated, and hydroxylated forms.
name: Amino Acids and Derivatives

description: Purine and pyrimidine nucleotides, nucleosides, and bases including ATP, ADP, AMP, GMP, CMP, UMP, and their derivatives. Also includes modified nucleosides and bases.
name: Nucleotides, Nucleosides, and Bases

description: Sugars, sugar derivatives, organic acids from central metabolism including TCA cycle intermediates, and other small molecule metabolites.
name: Carbohydrates and Organic Acids

description: Miscellaneous metabolites including vitamins, hormones, drug metabolites, environmental compounds, and other bioactive molecules not fitting into major lipid or amino acid categories.
name: Other Metabolites and Xenobiotics

description: Bone mineral density (BMD) by DEXA scan, fracture history and location, osteoporosis diagnosis and treatment, grip strength, joint replacement (knee, hip), kyphosis/scoliosis, back pain, musculoskeletal exam findings, and functional mobility assessments.
name: Bone Density and Musculoskeletal Health

description: Bone mineral density measurements from DEXA scans including femoral neck, total hip, lumbar spine, and other anatomical sites. Includes BMD values, T-scores, and scan-related variables.
name: Bone Mineral Density and DEXA Scans

description: History of fractures and broken bones at any anatomical site including hip, spine, wrist, ankle, and other locations. Includes fracture circumstances, dates, locations, and pathologic fractures.
name: Fractures and Broken Bones

description: Diagnosis and treatment of osteoporosis, osteopenia, and other bone diseases. Includes medications for bone health, bone density status, and related conditions affecting bone metabolism.
name: Osteoporosis and Bone Disease

description: Hand grip strength measurements including dominant and non-dominant hands, multiple trials, test completion status, and reasons for non-completion. Includes standardized grip strength assessments.
name: Grip Strength and Hand Function

description: Scoliosis, kyphosis, and other spinal curvature abnormalities including degree measurements, age of onset, treatment with bracing or surgery, and associated complications.
name: Spinal Deformity (Scoliosis and Kyphosis)

description: Hip and knee joint replacements, orthopedic surgical procedures, and related interventions for musculoskeletal conditions.
name: Joint Replacement and Orthopedic Surgery

description: Back pain symptoms, lower back pain episodes, pain characteristics, duration, and associated leg pain or neurological symptoms.
name: Back Pain and Spinal Symptoms

description: Rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, joint pain, stiffness, swelling, and other arthritic conditions affecting joints throughout the body.
name: Arthritis and Joint Disease

description: Connective tissue disorders, rheumatologic conditions, lupus, scleroderma, and related autoimmune conditions affecting musculoskeletal system.
name: Connective Tissue and Rheumatologic Disease

description: Muscle strength testing, motor function assessments, range of motion, muscle weakness, atrophy, and neuromuscular examination findings.
name: Muscle Strength and Motor Function

description: General musculoskeletal symptoms, conditions, and physical examination findings not covered by other specific categories. Includes musculoskeletal disease classifications and general assessments.
name: General Musculoskeletal Conditions

description: COPD exacerbations and respiratory-related musculoskeletal symptoms including treatment episodes and breathing difficulties.
name: Respiratory and COPD-Related Musculoskeletal

name: PR Interval

description: Duration of ventricular depolarization from QRS onset to offset
name: QRS Interval

description: Duration from QRS onset to T wave end, measuring ventricular repolarization
name: QT Interval

description: Heart rate corrected QT interval using various formulas (Bazett, Fridericia)
name: QT Corrected

description: Electrical axis of ventricular depolarization in degrees
name: QRS Axis

description: Electrical axis of atrial depolarization
name: P Axis

description: Electrical axis of ventricular repolarization
name: T Axis

description: Regular atrial rhythm with sawtooth flutter waves
name: Atrial Flutter

description: Early atrial beats originating outside the sinus node
name: Premature Atrial Contractions

description: Early ventricular beats originating from ventricular tissue

description: Increased left ventricular muscle mass detected by voltage criteria

description: Increased right ventricular muscle mass
name: Right Ventricular Hypertrophy

description: Complete or incomplete conduction block in right bundle branch

name: T Wave Abnormalities

description: Presence of artificial cardiac pacemaker

description: Measures of beat-to-beat heart rate variation
